title: The Currency Crisis
subtitle: The exchange rate falls. Confidence follows.

turns.prompt: The currency drops 30% in a week. Import prices surge. Savers rush to convert holdings to foreign cash.

choices.label: Seek an IMF bailout with strict conditions

delayed.headline: IMF conditions bite — subsidies and wages cut

choices.label: Impose capital controls and limit withdrawals
choices.reaction: Bank queues form. Black markets flourish.

choices.label: Default on foreign debt and reset
choices.reaction: Bondholders rage. Borrowing costs spike globally for your name.

turns.prompt: Inflation accelerates. Supermarket prices change between morning and evening.

choices.label: Hike interest rates aggressively
choices.flavour: Pain now to save the currency.
choices.reaction: Currency steadies slightly. Mortgages break households.

choices.label: Subsidise essential imports — food and medicine
choices.flavour: Shield shelves from the exchange rate.
choices.reaction: Basic goods stay affordable — briefly. Reserves drain.

choices.label: Allow dollarisation for contracts and savings
choices.flavour: Let foreign money become official money.
choices.reaction: Dollars circulate openly. Policy levers shrink.

turns.prompt: General strike. Small businesses cannot import inputs. The middle class empties savings accounts.

choices.label: Index wages partially to inflation
choices.reaction: Payday feels normal. Employers quietly halve hiring plans.

choices.label: Sell national gold and strategic reserves
choices.reaction: Markets rally — for a day. Strategic weakness lingers.

choices.label: Crash devalue to boost exports
choices.reaction: Factories receive orders. Import-dependent cities suffer.

turns.prompt: Foreign suppliers demand payment upfront in hard currency. There is nowhere left to hide from the balance sheet.

memory_callbacks.text: IMF conditions you accepted now forbid the subsidies people need.
Nobody will lend after the default — at any rate you can afford.
Capital controls you imposed are strangling the importers you need.

choices.label: Peg the currency and defend it with reserves
choices.flavour: Draw a line in the sand.
choices.reaction: Confidence returns — until reserves run out.

choices.label: Join a regional currency union
choices.reaction: Exchange volatility fades. Policy moves to a committee abroad.

turns.prompt: Final reckoning. What monetary future did you leave?

choices.label: Enshrine fiscal discipline and independent central banking
choices.reaction: Inflation memories fade slowly. Growth stays cautious — like everyone else.

choices.label: Accept a weaker currency with active management
choices.reaction: Exports hum. Imported goods stay expensive.

choices.label: Trade sovereignty for stability in a larger bloc
choices.reaction: Crises become collective problems. Voice feels diluted — but at least it's shared.
